title: VS Code becomes a certifiable G3 conformance client via its real spawn seam
context: dec_ce86ca9cec certified only the cli/mcp/ci profile and required every other client set to fail closed as unsupported-client-profile, because labels are not executable conformance evidence. The VS Code Hunch Memory panel became the flagship human surface (activate/demote/withdraw/retire), so it needs real certification — not an argument that it 'just delegates to the CLI'.
decision: G3 adapter conformance now supports a table of certifiable client profiles (G3_CONFORMANCE_PROFILES), each mapping a human-selected client set to ONE executable end-to-end fixture. Two profiles exist: ci/cli/mcp (unchanged) and ci/cli/mcp/vscode. The vscode profile is certified by executing the extension's REAL spawn seam — vscode-extension/src/spawnCore.ts (runHunchWith: the exact arg quoting, Windows npm-shim cmd.exe handling, and result shaping the panel uses), extracted vscode-free so a plain node:test can drive it against an npm-style shim. The fixture asserts byte-identical deterministic receipt hashes across CLI, MCP, check, and the seam, plus zero private leakage through the seam's public-only output. Every other client set still fails closed. All other dec_ce86ca9cec doctrine (append-only, tamper protection, authority:none, g3_passed:false, human-only activation) carries forward unchanged.
consequences: A future G3-class plan may name the four-client profile and obtain a real passed receipt including vscode.
The extension's spawn behavior is now under executable certification — changing spawnCore semantics breaks the conformance fixture, not just the panel.
The fixture itself caught a receipt contamination during development (planting the shim inside the fixture repo changed the working-snapshot hash) — evidence the equality check has teeth.
alternatives_rejected: Certify vscode by argument (it delegates to the CLI) — rejected: labels are not executable conformance evidence; that is the exact rejected alternative of the superseded decision
Replicate the extension's spawn logic inside the fixture — rejected: certifies a copy, not the client; the real seam was extracted vscode-free instead
A separate vscode-only fixture — rejected: certification is receipt EQUALITY, so all four surfaces must run in one fixture against one snapshot
